author: Dr. Lizard
title: What is the gold standard for identifying amyloid precursor proteins?
answer: Mass spectrometry-based analysis of amyloid-containing tissue is the gold standard for identifying precursor proteins.
detail: Laser microdissection mass spectrometry has reported sensitivity of 88 percent and specificity of 96 percent for amyloid typing.
Mass spectrometry is superior to immunohistochemistry, which showed nonspecific results in 24 percent of cases.
Immunohistochemistry for transthyretin or serum amyloid A should be performed if kappa and lambda stains are negative.
Accurate amyloid typing is essential because AL amyloidosis requires chemotherapy while ATTR amyloidosis needs different management approaches.
releaseNote: Mass spectrometry-based analysis is the gold standard for identifying amyloid precursor proteins, offering high sensitivity and specificity, and is superior to immunohistochemistry in certain cases.
